seoTitle: Can Smartsheet Send Automated Emails? Yes, Here's How
metaDescription: Set up automated email reminders in Smartsheet using the Automation menu, date-driven triggers, conditional filters, and personalized message content.
intro: Smartsheet can absolutely send automated emails, and setting it up takes just a few steps inside the Automation menu. This guide covers building a workflow with triggers and conditions, configuring date-driven reminders, and personalizing the message so your team gets timely, relevant alerts.

steps.heading: Open the Automation menu
steps.body: From your active sheet, open the Automation menu to launch the visual workflow builder.

steps.heading: Choose your trigger
steps.body: Set up the basic anatomy of the workflow by defining triggers, conditions, and actions, starting with a date-driven rule.

steps.heading: Configure reminder timing
steps.body: Set the reminder to fire a specific number of days before a milestone deadline.

steps.heading: Personalize the message
steps.body: Pull dynamic row information like Task Name and Status into the email subject or body text.

steps.heading: Add conditional filters
steps.body: Restrict the automation so it only triggers when a task's progress field matches a specific state, and review delivery permissions for internal versus external recipients.

faq.question: Does Smartsheet support automated email notifications?
faq.answer: Yes, the Automation menu lets you build workflows that send emails based on triggers like approaching due dates.

faq.question: Can I personalize the automated email content?
faq.answer: Yes, you can dynamically pull row data like Task Name and Status into the subject or body of the email.

faq.question: Can automated emails go to people outside my organization?
faq.answer: Delivery controls let you choose whether messages are restricted to internal users or sent to external stakeholders.

faq.question: Can I limit reminders to only overdue tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, conditional filters let you target only tasks matching a specific status, such as incomplete or overdue.
